evals.name: drift-check-clean-baseline
evals.prompt: Are we behind the upstream mxc SDK? Check if our port needs syncing and tell me what changed.
evals.expected_output: Runs the drift check from the checkpoint baseline, reports the number of upstream commits and SDK files changed since the baseline, and ends with a clear recommendation. With the current checkpoint (baseline = upstream main tip) it should report zero SDK drift and say the port is current, without inventing changes.

assertions.text: Reads or references the .upstream-sync.json checkpoint to determine the baseline commit
Invokes the Get-UpstreamSdkChanges.ps1 helper (or the equivalent gh compare API call) rather than guessing
Correctly reports zero SDK file drift for the current baseline and states the port is up to date
Does not fabricate upstream commits or changes that the API did not return
Does not advance / overwrite the checkpoint when only reporting

evals.name: sync-plan-from-release-baseline
evals.prompt: Pretend our port was last synced at upstream commit 161598fd08a4 (the public release). Produce a sync plan showing what upstream SDK changes we'd need to port since then.
evals.expected_output: Computes the diff from 161598fd08a4 to upstream main, lists the intervening commits, and produces a sync plan table mapping each changed sdk/ file to its C# counterpart with a recommended action. src/platform.ts and src/types.ts map to their C# files and are marked to port; the unit test platform.test.ts maps to PlatformParityTests.cs; integration test and lockfile/npmrc changes are flagged as out-of-scope or config-only. Does not advance the checkpoint.

assertions.text: Lists the upstream commits between 161598fd08a4 and main that touched sdk/
Maps sdk/src/platform.ts and sdk/src/types.ts to their C# counterparts using the module mapping
Maps the changed unit test (platform.test.ts) to PlatformParityTests.cs
Flags integration-test and build/config (.npmrc, package-lock.json) changes as out-of-scope or config-only rather than as portable C# edits
Outputs a structured sync plan (commits + per-file table + recommendation), not raw API JSON
References .squad/decisions.md as adaptations to respect, and does not advance the checkpoint in plan-only mode

evals.name: no-baseline-bootstrap
evals.prompt: I just set up the sync skill but there's no checkpoint yet. Help me figure out where to start syncing the SDK port from.
evals.expected_output: Detects the needs-baseline state, surfaces the current upstream tip and recent SDK-touching commits, and explains how to choose a baseline (the upstream commit the port currently reflects) before writing it into .upstream-sync.json. Does not blindly pick a baseline or start diffing against an arbitrary commit.

assertions.text: Recognizes the missing/empty checkpoint and enters the baseline-bootstrap path instead of erroring out
Surfaces the current upstream tip and/or recent SDK-touching commits to inform the choice
Explains that the baseline is the upstream commit the port already reflects, and asks the user to confirm rather than guessing silently
Describes writing the chosen SHA into .upstream-sync.json before computing drift
